description: §6.1.3 V5-strict bare-name discipline (codes): a local declaration may not reuse a registered state-cell name. `<count> = 0` registers the cell; the later `let count = 5` inside a logic-block function shadows that name → E-NAME-COLLIDES-STATE. This is the SPEC's own worked example (SPEC.md:2054-2062) transcribed. Asserted AS an error. Incidental W-PROGRAM-001 / E-DG-002 ignored by superset matching.
rationale: §6.1.3 (SPEC.md:2065): "E-NAME-COLLIDES-STATE (compile error): A local identifier declaration uses the same name as a registered state cell in scope. Local names cannot shadow state names." §1.6 (SPEC.md:184) grounds why: "Bare names in expressions are LOCAL identifiers only. They do NOT resolve to reactive state cells. Local names cannot shadow registered state names." A local `count` would shadow access to the cell's name in expression positions, which the compiler refuses (§6.1 / SPEC.md:6000) — the canonical read is `@count`.
